summary.name: Liquid Small-Cap Tilt Equal-Weight Top-Traded
summary.author: Louis
summary.description: The strategy is a systematic small cap tilt within liquid US equities, seeking a size premium while controlling implementation risk through liquidity filtering and diversified equal weighting. Each month it constructs a cross sectional universe by first restricting to stocks with fundamental coverage and a price above 10, then ranking by dollar trading volume to identify the 200 most actively traded names, and finally selecting the 10 lowest market capitalization stocks within that liquid subset
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a systematic small cap tilt within liquid US equities, seeking a size premium while controlling implementation risk through liquidity filtering and diversified equal weighting. Each month it constructs a cross sectional universe by first restricting to stocks with fundamental coverage and a price above 10, then ranking by dollar trading volume to identify the 200 most actively traded names, and finally selecting the 10 lowest market capitalization stocks within that liquid subset. The intended edge is exposure to small cap factor returns while avoiding the least tradable microcaps that typically carry higher transaction costs and event risk.

Portfolio decisions are made on a monthly schedule with an hourly data feed and a short warm up period to enable immediate deployment. On each rebalance date at 10 00 local market time, the strategy invests equally across the selected 10 stocks and forces the portfolio to match those weights, implying full investment and a complete refresh of names as the selection set changes. Risk controls are limited to the embedded liquidity and price filters plus equal weighting; there are no explicit volatility targets, drawdown limits, sector constraints, or stop loss exits.

raw.name: Liquid Small-Cap Tilt Equal-Weight Top-Traded

author.name: Louis
author.bio: I am Louis and I have a passion for options, trading, and computer science. Algorithmic trading is a great way to combine these things.
